pages.latin_text: Jēmima anas erat quae in fundō habitābat.
pages.english_text: Jemima was a duck who lived on a farm.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_003.jpg] The farmyard scene: the farmer's wife at the door, Jemima among hens, ducks, and chicks in the yard.

pages.latin_text: Jēmima ōva sua ipsa fovēre volēbat.
pages.english_text: Jemima wanted to hatch her own eggs.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_004.jpg] Jemima in the barn looking frustrated, standing apart from the other hens.

pages.latin_text: Sed uxor agricolae ōva semper auferēbat.
pages.english_text: But the farmer's wife always took the eggs away.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_005.jpg] The farmer's wife carrying eggs away while Jemima watches.

pages.latin_text: Itaque Jēmima fundum relīquit. Nīdum sēcrētum quaerēbat.
pages.english_text: So Jemima left the farm. She was looking for a secret nest.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_006.jpg] Jemima setting off from the farm, wearing her bonnet and shawl.

pages.latin_text: Super collem volāvit, locum quaerēns.
pages.english_text: She flew over the hill, looking for a place.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_008.jpg] Jemima in flight, wings spread, soaring over green hills.

pages.latin_text: In silvam dēscendit. Digitālēs ubīque flōrēbant.
pages.english_text: She landed in a wood. Foxgloves bloomed everywhere.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_010.jpg] Jemima standing in a shady clearing among tall foxglove flowers.

pages.latin_text: Vir elegāns ibi sedēbat, acta diurna legēns.
pages.english_text: An elegant gentleman sat there, reading a newspaper.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_011.jpg] The fox on a tree stump, reading a newspaper. Jemima approaches.

pages.latin_text: Jēmima eī omnia nārrāvit. Ille attentē audīvit.
pages.english_text: Jemima told him everything. He listened carefully.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_012.jpg] Jemima talking earnestly to the fox, who listens with exaggerated sympathy.

pages.latin_text: "Locum optimum nōvī," inquit vulpēs, subrīdēns.
pages.english_text: "I know a very good place," said the fox, smiling.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_013.jpg] The fox leading Jemima to a tumbledown shed in the woods.

pages.latin_text: Tugurium plūmīs plēnum erat. Plūmae mollissimae!
pages.english_text: The shed was full of feathers. Very soft feathers!
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_015.jpg] Jemima nestled in a deep bed of feathers inside the shed.

pages.latin_text: Jēmima novem ōva in nīdō plūmeō posuit.
pages.english_text: Jemima laid nine eggs in the feathery nest.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_014.jpg] The fox politely closing the shed door, locking Jemima inside with her eggs.

pages.latin_text: Vulpēs valdē comis erat. Cotīdiē cibum dabat.
pages.english_text: The fox was very polite. He gave her food every day.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_016.jpg] The fox bowing politely to Jemima, who looks flattered.

pages.latin_text: Subrīdēbat et dē ōvīs rogābat.
pages.english_text: He smiled and asked about the eggs.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_017.jpg] The fox sitting close to Jemima, head tilted, smiling. Too attentive.

pages.latin_text: Tum vulpēs herbās et cēpās poposcit.
pages.english_text: Then the fox asked for herbs and onions.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_018.jpg] The fox by a gate, asking Jemima for sage and onions.

pages.latin_text: Jēmima salviam et cēpās laeta collēgit. Nihil intellegēbat.
pages.english_text: Jemima happily gathered sage and onions. She understood nothing.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_019.jpg] Jemima gathering herbs in the farm garden.

pages.latin_text: In fundō, Jēmima Kep canem invēnit.
pages.english_text: On the farm, Jemima met Kep the collie dog.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_020.jpg] Jemima talking to Kep the collie by the farmhouse door.

pages.latin_text: Eī omnia nārrāvit. Kep statim intellēxit.
pages.english_text: She told Kep everything. Kep understood at once.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_021.jpg] Kep trotting off with purpose after hearing Jemima's story.

pages.latin_text: Jēmima herbās ad vulpem portāvit. Ille laetissimus erat.
pages.english_text: Jemima brought the herbs to the fox. He was very happy.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_022.jpg] Jemima carrying her bundle of herbs through the countryside.

pages.latin_text: Sed Kep cum duōbus canibus vēnātōriīs vēnit!
pages.english_text: But Kep came with two foxhound dogs!
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_024.jpg] Kep arriving with two foxhound dogs, running toward the fox's lair.

pages.latin_text: Canēs vulpem fugāvērunt! In silvam fūgit.
pages.english_text: The dogs chased the fox away! He fled into the woods.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_025.jpg] The foxhound dogs chasing. The fox is fleeing.

pages.latin_text: Jēmima salva erat. Sed canēs ōva ēderant.
pages.english_text: Jemima was safe. But the dogs had eaten her eggs.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_027.jpg] Jemima walking home sadly with Kep and the foxhounds.

pages.latin_text: Posteā Jēmima quattuor anāticulās ēdūxit. Quattuor tantum.
pages.english_text: Later, Jemima hatched four ducklings. Only four.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: image_028.jpg] Jemima with four small yellow ducklings at the farm.
